finding_results.notes: The post-cafeteria section now carries a fresh operational consequence. Priya's technical utility produces a concrete rule change, Marcus's decline forces Noor into immediate action, Tomas's privileged meal remains visible, and the ending turns on Lena consolidating all survivors into the cafeteria.

The chapter now states 39 degrees today, 38.5 tomorrow, and 38 on the final day, matching the half-degree decline and the continuity sheet's high-thirties endpoint. The late chapter consequence is also tied to a lost heat loop rather than contradictory warmer figures.

finding_results.notes: Min's decline now stays on-page after Elise's blows. The passage tracks loss of motor control, unequal eye response, blood from the ear, wet breathing, whole-body tightening, loss of bladder control, and the final failed breaths instead of summarizing the death with elapsed time and diagnosis.
Marcus's scene now stays on his failing body and the manual-override regret. The repeated calloused-hands/server-racks identity inventory is gone.
Only the Ember message itself remains. The narration no longer repeats separate commentary passages about Ember's unchanged helpful register.
The cafeteria sequence now enters through what has changed: narrowed portions, reserve bars, and Noor's ration split. It no longer restates Lena's full three-meal schedule from scratch.
The chapter keeps a single visible-breath image at the open and tracks later cold through the thermostat math and the lost heat loop, instead of repeatedly proving the same ambient condition.
The three-item register inventory is removed. Ember appears through the message itself rather than a repeated list of stylistic traits.
The chapter no longer uses the recurring Haruki/Diego/Yuki death-tally construction. Attrition is registered through witnessed killings, the Ember occupancy note, and the forced consolidation ending.
Validation pass found 0 prose lines containing em dashes after the rewrite.
Validation pass found 6 sentence-openers beginning with 'She', well below the packet threshold.
Validation pass found 0 uses of 'something'. Vague hedges were replaced with specific physical and operational nouns.
The chapter no longer names the corridor corpse as Dae-jung or assigns any conflicting job title, removing the Chapter 14 side of the role contradiction.

summary: Chapter 14 now lands its authoritarian state shift through a concrete late consequence: Priya's monitoring leads Lena to collapse the survivors into the cafeteria before nightfall. The revision also puts Min's death fully on-page, reconciles the temperature drop to the continuity sheet, preserves the required Priya/Marcus/Tomas/cold beats, and removes the packet's repeated schedule, register, diction, and punctuation tics.
